title: centaurea luristanica
intro: Centaurea luristanica, also known as Luristan cornflower or blue knapweed, is a flowering plant in the sunflower family, characterized by its blue or purple flowers and coarse, hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Centaurea luristanica include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system, making it a potential natural remedy for colds and flu. Centaurea luristanica can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, and its leaves and flowers can be used in salves or ointments for topical application.
